reviews.review_msg: The midterms were somewhat similar to the homework and quizzes so pay attention to those. Does not require a textbook but you have to pay for WebAssign. Really helpful during office hours and the YouTube video are very similar to lectures. If you can, do take a class with him.

I was in the Prime section for Calculus 2, and I had Jamie Muholland and he is an amazing professor with great videos and great lectures. However, the tests were quite challenging, since I always loved Math and calculus since high school, I found the tests to be fair but they were quite conceptual and required a fairly deep understanding of math.

Do not let my grade reflect how amazing of a prof this guy is. I went to the calc workshop ONCE the day before the final and he answered my question so thoroughly and without any rush even though it was PACKED. He made learning calculus feel much less daunting.

I like how he has videos posted on youtube that covers the material which helps a lot if you don't want to go to lectures. The assignments that are for grades also force you to do them, but are very helpful to understand the material and it's hard to slack off in the class
He explains math in a way that makes people understand it better. Although the final exam was long, this class is not curved. The prof and TAs always tried to give you good marks.

Professor Mulholland is very nice and respected and deservedly so but I believe his method of teaching certain concepts makes it a bit difficult for students to understand, make sure you expose yourself to some calculus before taking this class. Also I would highly suggest watching the video lectures on youtube the night before lectures

Explains everything super clearly, reasonable amount of homework and plenty of practice material for exams. Dr. Mullholand was an all-round amazing prof! If you took calculus in high school, just know that calc 1 goes into much more depth and you will still need to put it in lots of effort to do well.
Jamie is one of the best profs at SFU. Would recommend taking any MATH course with him. Super organized and explains really well. Clear instructions and average tests.

Jamie Mulholland is a great prof for 152 . He is organized, caring, and has online videos. His videos are pretty good and allow you to go back on stuff at your own pace. Assignments and exams, are fair and marked on time.
Some of his explanations were so fire that I almost shed a tear, would take again!.

Jamie Mullholand is an amazing professor, and I left his class with no complaints.
Pros:
organized and On Time
-clear grading criteria
-answers any questions , makes sure you understand content
-pretty good explanations
-He explains to make sure you understand the concept/theory

IF U CAN TAKE MATH WITH THIS PROF

From listening to his lectures and talking with Jamie it is very obvious that he cares about what he is teaching. He covers all the material but still takes the time to thoroughly explain all the concepts. I recommend taking a course with Jamie whenever possible.

The best prof one can ever have. He explains really well and uses online tools for good visualisation. He has youtube videos so no worries if you miss a lecture. Addresses queries or doubts really quick and efficiently.
Only bad thing is that you need to purchase webassign access for around 100$. If you don't get into his class just watch his videos.

Jamie Mulholland is an amazing prof. He explains the material very clearly and does a great job explaining the concepts behind concepts when introducing them, so you know WHY things work the way they do. He also puts up his lectures on YouTube so you can watch them if you miss a day or dont quite get it yet. Course includes weekly in-class quizzes.

- has online yt vids if u ever skip (try not to)
- exams are all fair, and as long as you do the web assign without cheating and crowdmark without cheating ur fine for the tests
- has lots of exam resources
- weekly quizzes are very easy if you do your homework
- full 50 min lectures
- do practice exams that he gives

Very nice, very approachable prof, even if you aren't in his class. He takes time to explain in a very understandable manner; understandable why he's won national excellence in teaching awards before. His YouTube series are also extraordinarily helpful!

Jamie is wonderful, high quality lectures, clear concise notes with many practise examples to prepare you for midterms and the final. Overall he's just very knowledgable and pleasant to listen to, so go to his office hours if you need help, you wont regret it.
Beware of the two mark multiple choice questions though lol

Jamie Mulholland is a splendid man with a 99th percentile ability in teaching mathematics. He subbed for my teacher when he went on strike for MACM201. Instantly, me and many others understood the material much better. Jamie is a systematic and peaceful lecturer. Thank you Jamie.

Great professor explains the 'why' behind the math and doesn't just do it, which means that you have to memorize very little and majority of course 'makes sense'. Office hours are also very helpful- puts you on right direction for the questions.
Great teacher, passionate about his teaching, ATEND LECTURES

Great prof. Explains things well, stays on track and doesn't fall behind. He is also very accommodating if you have a reason for missing a quiz. Overall, kind, caring, effective at teaching. Only thing that I would say is final exam was VERY HARD much harder than the practice exams. However, they curved the grades and it ended up fine.
very good at explaining concepts and cares for the students. tests are difficult but weekly quizzes were okay. lots of self-studying and practice were needed, but homework was manageable

Goated prof. His teaching style is very streamlined and organized. The pace of the course itself was also good. It was especially nice having him answer personal questions during 150 seminars, since his explanations are very clear and concise. He uses a variety of technology in his lectures too, which make them a whole lot more visual and engaging.

Amazing professors who explains the topics clearly, has a sense of humor, and is engaging enough so you don't lose focus during lectures. Of course any Math course is going to be difficult so studying outside of lectures is important. Weekly assignments were generally harder or of the same difficulty as the material on the midterms + final.
Best ever math prof, so good at explaining

Great teacher, goes over concepts slowly and carefully, making them pretty easy to understand. Midterms are exactly what you expect, so as long as you do the assignments, you can be prepared for both the exams and the quizzes. During seminars, he answers any question you might have and shows you multiple ways to solve questions.

I don't know many people who enjoy taking math courses, but Jamie was by far the best math professor I have ever witnessed. Amazing online lectures, plenty of tutorial time and his exams were more than fair. If you have the chance to take Jamie for any math course, I would HIGHLY recommend!

DUDE I DON'T EVEN KNOW HOW TO TELL YOU THIS but if you ever get a chance ti take a class with Dr.Jamie JUST DO IT I MEAN IT!! he provides lectures on YouTube so no need to attend class,ATTEND f his office hours. he is so sweet and so caring. whatever questions you have he will answer it . I LOVE JAMIEEE PLZ TAKE HIS CLASs if U COULD
Jamie's MACM 201 lecture videos are A+ grade. They will save you from other profs who can't finish the material in 50min, or simply can't explain clearly.

TAKE HIM FOR MATH155!! DONT THINK JUST DO IT. All the exams were really easy it was definitely easy to over study for an exam. he did lectures in person and also went home and recorded them online separately for students who couldn't attend. He made everything so simple and easy. 10/10 would take any class with him again.

Math 155 barely had integration and had more calc 3 stuff. Jamie made the class easy. for exams just know how to do every single example in the notes. Jamie is an amazing prof as he explains everything clearly and his pre lecture videos were 100 times better then all the other profs

Jamie was an awesome prof. Take math155 with him especially if your math skills are poor or you are redoing the course. Before I took calculus 2 with Jamie I always struggled with math. At the end of the semester I finally not only got an A, but understood the content completely.
I think pandemic got the best of him. Sub par. Doesnt care. Doesnt reply to my email.

Jamie was very organized & accessible outside of class. His lectures are great, clear examples & explanation. He doesn't waste any time. Most of what you need to know is in lecture and assignments. Discrete 2 is a difficult course, but Jamie is fair and there were no surprises. Effort will take you far in his class. Thank you Jamie!
Best Math professor! Instead of watching Randall Pyke's videos, which are appalling, watch Jamies!
The only problem was that the assignments were extra and not useful. He uses WebAssign and it's enough work. Also, you really need a high mark to get A because the average is high.
Great professor, give Jamie some slack. Math is a hard subject to teach, but he is able to do it wonderfully. Would definitely take his classes again! :)
Midterms are doable (if you put in the work) but the timing of them is not. Not worth to take this class with this prof again even though he was rated almost a 5/5. Such a shame.
THE GOAT.

Gives good lectures that are only hindered by the logistics of 8 am classes. Homework is online and fair enough. For me he was the first taste of university education and looking back, I wish more profs are on his level of teaching ability and passion because most of them are far worse.
In prof Jungic class currently, I use Jamies vids to help me understand better. Jamie must teach cal 1 for first semester students as taking it with any1 else is horrible and demotivating.
Engaging and clearly communicates. Thoroughly explains questions in an easy to understand manner. Here is award winning and I highly recommend him.

THE best professor for calculus!! I hated calculus going into it and wasnt really looking forward to this one but he made me fall in love with calculus like I never knew possible. Very clear explanations and I learned twice as much as I did in calc I because he made it easier to learn. I highly recommended!!

He is great at explaining things, he gives a good amount of material to study for quizzes/tests, not all of his lectures are mandatory and he has a youtube channel (you can find it by searching his name in youtube) full of tutorial videos for everything in 150 and 151. Pretty much everything you could ask for in a teacher.
amazing teacher
Cool teacher
